name: Otterbourne
overview: Otterbourne is a village in Hampshire about four miles south of Winchester and eight miles north of Southampton, with a population of around 1,539 (2011 census). Recorded in the Domesday Book as 'Otreburne' (otter stream), it sits near the River Itchen and has a traditional character mixing thatched cottages with modern housing. The focal point is the Grade II listed 13th-century St Matthew's Church, and the village has good amenities plus quick access to the M3.
character: An idyllic, well-served Itchen-side village with strong community facilities and a desirable location between Winchester and Southampton.
amenities: Nisa Local convenience store and Post Office
Three pubs: the White Horse Inn, the Otter and the Old Forge
Close to Shawford station and the M3

prices.summary: A popular family village with detached homes and period cottages; values supported by the Outstanding-rated primary, Thornden catchment and easy commuting. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walks along the River Itchen and Itchen Navigation
Cycling and walking towards the South Downs
placesToEat: The Old Forge (family pub with dining)
pros: Three pubs and a convenience store
Excellent commuting via M3 and Shawford/Winchester rail
cons: Main Road and proximity to the M3 bring some traffic noise
No secondary school in the village
Popular location means competitive house prices
whoItSuits: Families wanting a well-served Hampshire village with a top primary and fast links to Winchester, Southampton and London.
